title: PTD Today — Media

items.title: AWS Power Hour: Generative AI For Developers | S1 E1 | Exam Overview and FM Integrations

ai.summary: Based on the available description, this episode reviews the exam format and study strategies while guiding listeners through designing resilient generative AI architectures. Hosts cover building data processing pipelines for foundation models, implementing vector stores for RAG systems, applying prompt engineering strategies, and analyzing sample exam questions alongside real-world solutions.
bullets: Overview of exam format and recommended study approaches.
Guidance on designing resilient GenAI architectures.
How to build data processing pipelines tailored to foundation models.
Implementing vector stores for retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems.
Prompt engineering techniques and analysis of sample exam questions with real-world solutions.
takeaways: Prioritize resilient architecture designs to support GenAI workloads in production environments.
Ensure data processing pipelines are planned and provisioned to feed foundation models reliably.
Allocate capacity and storage strategies for vector stores used in RAG deployments.
Factor prompt-engineering workflows into testing and operational processes to validate behavior under load.

items.title: re:Invent - Chalk Talk - Solving private IPv4 exhaustion using AWS Cloud WAN & Amazon VPC Lattice

ai.summary: Based on the available description… This session explains how to address private IPv4 exhaustion by combining AWS Cloud WAN with service insertion and Amazon VPC Lattice to centralize private NAT Gateways and AWS PrivateLink. The approach aims to maximize IP space utilization, reduce costs, and simplify network management across distributed environments.
bullets: Causes of private IPv4 exhaustion: limited address planning, architectural shifts, mergers, and containerized workloads
Solution combines AWS Cloud WAN (with service insertion) and Amazon VPC Lattice
Centralizes private NAT Gateways and AWS PrivateLink to conserve IPs
Benefits include better IP utilization, lower costs, and simplified network operations
Designed as a scalable, durable approach for distributed infrastructure
takeaways: Adopting Cloud WAN + VPC Lattice can relieve IPv4 scarcity and restore cloud scalability
Centralizing NAT and PrivateLink reduces per-VPC IP consumption and operating expense
Simplified network management lowers operational complexity for distributed environments
Provides a durable architecture to support mergers, architectural change, and IP‑hungry workloads

items.title: Evan Bates Praises Figure Skating's Next Generation

ai.summary: Based on the available description… Evan Bates praises the next generation of figure skating, saying the sport's future has never looked brighter. The short Team USA video centers that optimism around the Winter Olympics and directs viewers to Team USA's social channels.
bullets: Evan Bates expresses optimism about figure skating’s future.
Video ties the message to the Winter Olympics context.
Published by Team USA on YouTube with social cross-promotion links.
No transcript is provided in the post.
takeaways: Plan for increased streaming and CDN capacity for athlete highlight videos during the Winter Olympics window.
Prioritize low-latency delivery of short promotional clips across Team USA’s platforms (YouTube, Instagram, TikTok).
Monitor channel traffic and scale edge caching to ensure availability for event-driven spikes.
Ensure metadata and tagging for quick retrieval and playlisting of athlete-focused content.

items.title: How I turned $27K of land into $700/night luxury cabin rentals

ai.summary: Based on the available description, in 2015 full-time contractor Chris Broomfield bought a five-acre property in upstate New York intending to build Airbnb properties. Over the next three years he made weekend commutes from Connecticut to single-handedly build an A-frame cabin, added more cabins later, and as of September 2025 the property has generated over $2.1 million.
bullets: 2015: purchased a five-acre property in upstate New York to develop Airbnb rentals.
Chris Broomfield, a full-time contractor, commuted on weekends from Connecticut to build an A-frame cabin himself over three years.
Additional cabins were constructed after the initial build.
As of September 2025, the property has brought in over $2.1 million.
takeaways: A rural short-term rental project generated substantial revenue (over $2.1M by Sep 2025), indicating economic viability for similar decentralized hospitality developments.
Owner-driven, phased construction in rural areas can result in incremental increases in local electricity and connectivity demand as units are added.
Monitoring local short-term rental and vacation rental growth can help anticipate infrastructure needs (power capacity, broadband) in rural markets.

items.title: Restrukturyzacja a podatki. Rozpoznanie, obowiązki, konsekwencje | Monika Palmowska, Tomasz Regulski

ai.summary: Based on the available description… The episode explains how to view operational changes (e.g., moving production or distribution, transferring employees or contracts) from a transfer pricing and valuation perspective, what reporting obligations (TPR, MDR) may arise, and why timely documentation and solid valuations reduce future stress. Experts Monika Palmowska and Tomasz Regulski give practical examples, common pitfalls, and a checklist of actions for companies.
bullets: Defines restructuring in the transfer pricing context and how to identify material changes (including EBIT analysis).
Details reporting obligations, specifically Transfer Pricing Documentation (TPR) and Mandatory Disclosure Rules (MDR).
Highlights importance of timely, robust documentation and valuations to limit future compliance risk.
Reviews common valuation errors and practical examples like relocating production/distribution and transferring staff or contracts.
Provides a concluding checklist of actions for firms undergoing operational changes.
takeaways: Treat relocations or shifts in service/profile as potential TP restructuring events and analyze EBIT impact.
Prepare transfer pricing documentation and valuations proactively to meet TPR and MDR obligations and reduce audit exposure.
When transferring employees or contracts, assess changes to business profile and contractual terms to avoid misclassification.
Use the episode's checklist to guide documentation, valuation quality checks, and reporting timelines.

Title emphasizes Copilot as backup for your goals
Call to action: download and try via link in bio
Published on 2026-02-03
No transcript provided
takeaways: Vendor is actively promoting Copilot—consider taking the offered trial to assess relevance for your operations

items.title: The Most Expensive Mistake in AI (And How to Avoid It)

ai.summary: Based on the available description, Marcus Oliver of Google delta argues the most expensive AI mistake is rushing to code without a strategy rather than concerns about compute costs. He recommends escaping the "coding trap" by reimagining value chains and using multi-agent systems to drive real business outcomes.
bullets: Most costly AI mistake: rushing into coding without a strategy.
Focus on reimagining value chains before building models or systems.
Deploy multi-agent systems to achieve tangible business outcomes.
Business outcomes matter more than compute cost concerns.
Features Marcus Oliver, lead of Google delta (professional services).
takeaways: Prioritize strategy and value-chain analysis before provisioning data center or grid resources.
Assess AI projects by expected business outcomes to avoid wasted compute and infrastructure spend.
Design infrastructure to support multi-agent architectures where appropriate.
Coordinate with strategy/professional services teams to align deployments with business value.

items.title: DeepKeep: Secure your AI agents and applications end-to-end

ai.summary: Based on the available description, DeepKeep on Google Cloud is presented as a unified platform to secure AI agents, applications, and user interactions, providing visibility to eliminate shadow AI and protect infrastructure. The offering highlights AI Lens for interaction visibility and policy application, AI Red Teaming for stress-testing models and apps, and an AI Firewall that enforces real-time guardrails on prompts and responses.
bullets: Unified platform to secure AI agents, applications, and user interactions
AI Lens: visibility and policy enforcement across AI interactions
AI Red Teaming: stress-test applications and models against real-world attacks
AI Firewall: real-time guardrails for prompts and responses
Aims to eliminate shadow AI and protect infrastructure
takeaways: Centralized visibility and policy controls help reduce shadow AI and unmanaged risk
Regular red-teaming can identify vulnerabilities before production deployment
Real-time guardrails on prompts/responses reduce the risk of data exposure or malicious inputs
Adopting a consolidated AI security platform on Google Cloud can help protect infrastructure and AI usage

Simple call-to-action (link in bio) implies low-friction entry points—factor ease of onboarding and support requirements into rollout plans.

items.title: GeForce Garage - Summer of RTX Community Build

ai.summary: Based on the available description, NVIDIA’s GeForce Garage crowns the r/buildapc Summer of RTX community build winner, u/JeffersonPutnam, who assembled a small-form-factor GeForce RTX 5090 system. The video description lists full specs — Fractal Design Era 2 chassis, GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition, MSI MPG B850I Edge, Ryzen 7 9800X3D, 32GB DDR5-6000, Corsair SF1000 PSU and Corsair Nautilus 240 RS AIO — and links to the PCPartPicker list and GeForce social channels.
bullets: Winner: u/JeffersonPutnam’s SFF GeForce RTX 5090 build for the Summer of RTX contest
Host: GeForce host Dwight (Twitter: @_MightyPotato)
Full parts list linked: PCPartPicker build URL provided
Key components: Fractal Era 2, RTX 5090 Founders Edition, MSI MPG B850I Edge, Ryzen 7 9800X3D, 32GB DDR5-6000, Corsair SF1000, Corsair Nautilus 240 RS
GeForce social links and community portal included in description
takeaways: The RTX 5090 is being showcased in a compact desktop (SFF) configuration, highlighting consumer desktop deployment scenarios rather than server form factors
Build uses a 1,000W SFX PSU and a 240mm AIO, indicating significant power and cooling requirements to support high-end GPUs in compact enclosures

System pairs the RTX 5090 with a mainstream consumer platform (MSI B850I + Ryzen 7 9800X3D and DDR5-6000), not a workstation/server motherboard, signaling target audience and compatibility considerations
NVIDIA leverages community builds and social channels for product visibility—monitor community adoption and configuration trends for real-world deployment insights
